title: Smoky Chewy Brazilian Black Beans Stew
description: A hearty, post-workout Brazilian stew bursting with smoky flavor and chewy fermented black beans, finished with a deglaze and fresh lime for a bright finish.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add diced onion and cook until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic, smoked paprika, cumin, and oregano; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add diced tomatoes and deglaze the pot with dry white wine, scraping up browned bits from the bottom.
Pour in vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Add the fermented black beans and reduce heat to low; cover and let simmer for 30 minutes, allowing the beans to absorb the smoky flavors and thicken slightly.
Stir in lime juice, taste, and season with salt and pepper to preference.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro before serving.
Serve hot over a bed of brown rice or quinoa for a complete post-workout meal.
